title: Apple Human Interface Design Systems
subtitle: The principles behind iOS, macOS, and visionOS that have shaped a generation of design
description: A practical course on Apple's Human Interface Guidelines — how the world's most-imitated design language works in principle and execution. Covers Apple's design ethos, platform conventions across iOS / macOS / iPadOS / visionOS, typography (SF + Dynamic Type), color and dark mode, layout and the grid, controls and gestures, motion, accessibility, and how to apply HIG principles whether you're building Apple-platform apps or any product that aspires to feel native and intentional.
audience: Product designers, UI / UX engineers, indie developers shipping Apple-platform apps, anyone studying how Apple makes its design decisions.
prerequisites: Familiar with Apple ecosystem as a user
Have shipped or want to ship a designed interface
outcomes: Choose the right platform conventions for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, visionOS
Set typography and Dynamic Type correctly
Use color and dark mode the Apple way
Design layout, controls, and gestures that feel native

modules.title: The Apple design ethos
modules.objective: What Apple values and how it shows up.

lessons.title: Clarity, deference, depth — the three principles
Designing for humans, not for designers
Reading the HIG: how Apple writes its own rules

modules.title: Typography and layout
modules.objective: SF, Dynamic Type, and the grid.

lessons.title: SF Pro and the system fonts
Safe areas, layout margins, and adaptive layouts
Standard controls and when to use each
Gestures: tap, swipe, drag, long-press, force
Haptics and audio as design materials
iOS vs iPadOS vs macOS vs visionOS conventions
